verses.text: A man of God from Judah cries against Jeroboam's altar, foretelling that a king named Josiah will one day defile it, and gives a sign — the altar will split. God answers false worship with a clear, dated word of judgment, naming a deliverer not yet born.

A man of God from Judah cries against Jeroboam's altar, foretelling that a king named Josiah will one day defile it, and gives a sign — the altar will split. God answers false worship with a clear, dated word of judgment, naming a deliverer not yet born.

A man of God from Judah cries against Jeroboam's altar, foretelling that a king named Josiah will one day defile it, and gives a sign — the altar will split. God answers false worship with a clear, dated word of judgment, naming a deliverer not yet born.

Jeroboam stretches out his hand to seize the prophet, and it withers; at the man's prayer, it is restored. The hand raised against God's messenger is struck, then healed by grace. Yet even this double miracle does not turn the king's hardened heart.

Jeroboam stretches out his hand to seize the prophet, and it withers; at the man's prayer, it is restored. The hand raised against God's messenger is struck, then healed by grace. Yet even this double miracle does not turn the king's hardened heart.

Jeroboam stretches out his hand to seize the prophet, and it withers; at the man's prayer, it is restored. The hand raised against God's messenger is struck, then healed by grace. Yet even this double miracle does not turn the king's hardened heart.

The king offers reward and a meal, but the man of God refuses: God charged him not to eat or drink there, nor return the way he came. He keeps his commission exactly, declining the favour of the very court he rebuked. Obedience does not dine with what it must condemn.

An old prophet of Bethel hears of it, rides after the man of God, and finds him resting under an oak. The faithful messenger has done well — but the testing of his obedience is not yet over. The hardest trials sometimes come after the great public victory.

An old prophet of Bethel hears of it, rides after the man of God, and finds him resting under an oak. The faithful messenger has done well — but the testing of his obedience is not yet over. The hardest trials sometimes come after the great public victory.

The old prophet lies, claiming an angel told him to bring the man home to eat. And the man of God believes him and goes back, against God's plain word to himself. A false word, even from a fellow prophet, must never override what God has clearly told us.

The old prophet lies, claiming an angel told him to bring the man home to eat. And the man of God believes him and goes back, against God's plain word to himself. A false word, even from a fellow prophet, must never override what God has clearly told us.

As they eat, God's word comes through the old prophet himself: because you disobeyed, your body shall not reach your fathers' grave. The disobedient prophet is judged at the very table. To know God's command and set it aside, on any pretext, is a grave thing.

As they eat, God's word comes through the old prophet himself: because you disobeyed, your body shall not reach your fathers' grave. The disobedient prophet is judged at the very table. To know God's command and set it aside, on any pretext, is a grave thing.

As they eat, God's word comes through the old prophet himself: because you disobeyed, your body shall not reach your fathers' grave. The disobedient prophet is judged at the very table. To know God's command and set it aside, on any pretext, is a grave thing.

On the road a lion kills the man of God but stands by the body without devouring it or the donkey — no natural beast, but God's instrument. The judgment is unmistakable and exact. God's servants are held to His word, and disobedience in them is not lightly passed over.

On the road a lion kills the man of God but stands by the body without devouring it or the donkey — no natural beast, but God's instrument. The judgment is unmistakable and exact. God's servants are held to His word, and disobedience in them is not lightly passed over.

The old prophet hears, and knows: it is the man of God, slain for disobeying the LORD. The strange, restrained lion confirms it. God will be sanctified in those who come near Him; the messenger who would not keep the word he preached became a sermon himself.

The old prophet hears, and knows: it is the man of God, slain for disobeying the LORD. The strange, restrained lion confirms it. God will be sanctified in those who come near Him; the messenger who would not keep the word he preached became a sermon himself.

The old prophet buries him in his own grave, mourns 'Alas, my brother,' and charges that he be laid beside him, for the word he cried against Bethel's altar will surely come to pass. Even the deceiver now owns the truth of the dead man's prophecy.

After all this, Jeroboam still does not turn from his evil way but makes more false priests. Miracle, judgment, and warning leave him unchanged. A heart set on its sin can witness wonders and still refuse to repent — and so seal its own destruction.

After all this, Jeroboam still does not turn from his evil way but makes more false priests. Miracle, judgment, and warning leave him unchanged. A heart set on its sin can witness wonders and still refuse to repent — and so seal its own destruction.
